question_text: (a) Hint : Use Ellingham diagram (b) Hint : Oxides are easier to reduce. See Ellingham diagram. (c) Hint : Sulphide ore of copper contains iron as impurity which is removed as iron silicate (slag) FeO + SiO2 → FeSiO3 (Slag) (d) Hint : Carbon and hydrogen react with metals at high temperature to form carbides and hydrides respectively. (e) Hint : Ti reacts with iodine to form volatile TiI4 which decomposes at high temperature to give extra pure titanium. 89 General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements 23/04/18
